title: Principles of Surgical Asepsis and a Sterile Dressing Change
subtitle: A 58-year-old man is postoperative day 2 from abdominal surgery with a clean, intact midline incision that needs a sterile dressing change. The nurse must apply surgical asepsis correctly, keep the sterile field intact, and watch for early surgical-site infection.

patient.name: Carl Donovan
patient.admitDate: Postoperative day 2 after open abdominal surgery. Midline incision is clean, dry, and well-approximated. A sterile dressing change is ordered. Afebrile-trending; incisional pain controlled. No signs of infection at this time.
patient.room: Surgical floor; sterile dressing-change supplies at bedside

vitals.temp: Edges approximated, no drainage, mild expected tenderness

labs.r: Hyperglycemia raises SSI risk; optimize control.
Poor nutrition impairs healing/raises SSI risk.

labs.v: Edges approximated; no erythema or drainage.

nursesNote: Carl Donovan, 58, POD2 open abdominal surgery. Midline incision clean, dry, well-approximated; sterile dressing change ordered. Afebrile, pain controlled, no drainage. Glucose 185 and albumin 3.0 noted as healing/SSI-risk factors. Sterile supplies set up at bedside.
providerOrders: Sterile dressing change daily and PRN using surgical aseptic technique
Assess incision each shift; notify provider for erythema, purulent drainage, fever, or dehiscence
Optimize glycemic control; nutrition consult for low albumin

steps.title: Recognize Cues
steps.sub: What does a sterile dressing change require?

opts.text: A sterile field is required for the surgical-aseptic dressing change
Hand hygiene is done before and after; clean gloves remove the old dressing
opts.rat: Clean gloves for the soiled dressing.

opts.text: Sterile gloves are donned for the sterile portion of the procedure
Items below the waist or table level are considered contaminated
A 1-inch (2.5 cm) border around the sterile field is contaminated
Once set up, a sterile field stays sterile even if left unattended
opts.rat: False — an unattended field is contaminated.

steps.title: Analyze Cues
steps.sub: What maintains or breaks sterility — and the SSI risk?

opts.text: Reaching ACROSS the sterile field contaminates it
opts.rat: Never reach over a sterile field.

opts.text: Moisture wicking (strikethrough) from a wet field contaminates it
Turning your back on the sterile field contaminates it
opts.rat: Keep the field in view at all times.

opts.text: A clean, approximated incision with no drainage shows no current SSI
Hyperglycemia and low albumin increase surgical-site-infection risk
A set-up sterile field can be re-used for the next patient
opts.rat: False — never re-use a sterile field.

steps.title: Prioritize Hypotheses
steps.sub: Order the steps to BEGIN the dressing change

opts.text: Verify the order, gather supplies, and check the patient and allergies
Hand hygiene; explain the procedure; provide privacy and time analgesia
Don clean gloves; remove and discard the soiled dressing; assess the wound
opts.rat: #3 — Clean gloves for the old dressing.

opts.text: Perform hand hygiene again and set up the sterile field
Don sterile gloves and apply the sterile dressing
Keep all sterile items within the field and above waist level
Open the first package flap away from you, then the sides, then toward you
Keep sterile items in front of you and in view
Add sterile solution without splashing or soaking the field
Use sterile-to-sterile contact only
Don sterile gloves before touching sterile supplies or the wound
Reach over the sterile field with an ungloved arm
Set a sterile package on a wet surface
Turn away from the field to chart
Let the outer wrapper edge touch the sterile inner contents

steps.title: Take Action
steps.sub: Field is set and the wound is exposed — sequence the sterile application
steps.inst: Rank the order of actions. #1 = FIRST.

opts.text: Don sterile gloves
Clean the incision per order, one stroke per swab, clean-to-dirty
Apply the new sterile dressing without touching the wound-contact surface
Secure the dressing and ensure patient comfort
Remove gloves, perform hand hygiene, and document the wound
Hand hygiene was performed before and after the procedure
The nurse kept sterile items above the waist and in view
A strikethrough-soaked field was discarded and restarted
The incision was cleaned clean-to-dirty with a new swab each stroke
Wound appearance and patient tolerance were documented
The nurse reached across the sterile field to grab tape
The soiled dressing was removed with the sterile gloves meant for the new one
opts.rat: Use clean gloves for the old dressing.

opts.text: A dropped sterile gauze (below the waist) was used anyway
The sterile field was left unattended, then used
